# platform - SEO + GEO Remediation Playbook

**Target**: https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai  
**Backing service**: aicart/intelliverse-user-frontend (image `intelliverse-user-frontend:latest`)  
**Source repo (best guess)**: intelli-verse-x/intelliverse-user-frontend  
**Audit reference**: `reports/platform.md`  
**Generated**: 2026-05-17T21:00:00Z  
**Generated by**: gastown audits/crew/auditor (firecrawl + claude)

---

## 1. Executive summary

**Client-side SPA with critical SEO deficiencies** — Platform has HTTP 200 and basic title/description, but delivers virtually zero server-rendered content (only "Skip to main content" links visible in HTML). Current LLM-friendliness: 1/10. Missing: SSR/SSG, h1 tags, Open Graph, Twitter Cards, JSON-LD, HSTS, proper robots.txt/sitemap routing. **This is the main user platform site — discoverability is critical for brand SEO.**

**Quick fixes** (Phase 1, 3-4 hours): Fix robots.txt routing, enhance `index.html` metadata, add OG/Twitter/JSON-LD, security headers, /llms.txt → Improves to ~4-5/10

**Long-term fix** (Phase 2, 2-3 weeks): Migrate to Next.js SSR with hybrid rendering (public pages SSR, dashboard CSR) → Improves to 8-9/10

**Key consideration**: Platform is user-facing app with login/dashboard. Only **public pages** (landing, features, pricing) need indexing. Dashboard/account pages should be noindex.

**Projected impact**: 1/10 → 4-5/10 (Phase 1 metadata fixes) → 8-9/10 (Phase 2 SSR migration)

## 2. Current state recap

From `reports/platform.md`:

**PASS/OK**:
- ✓ **title tag** ([report#L9](reports/platform.md#L9)): "IntelliVerse X - Gaming & Ecommerce Platform" (descriptive)
- ✓ **meta description** ([report#L10](reports/platform.md#L10)): Present but too short (61 chars)
- ✓ **lang attribute** ([report#L19](reports/platform.md#L19)): `<html lang="en">`
- ✓ **Cache headers** ([report#L23](reports/platform.md#L23)): Good cache config

**FAIL/CRITICAL**:
- ✗ **Server-side rendering** ([report#L22](reports/platform.md#L22)): Client-side only SPA, essentially no content in HTML (CRITICAL)
- ✗ **h1 count** ([report#L18](reports/platform.md#L18)): No h1 tags in server HTML (CRITICAL)
- ✗ **robots.txt** ([report#L12](reports/platform.md#L12)): Returns HTML for different domain, routing misconfiguration (FAIL)
- ✗ **HSTS** ([report#L14](reports/platform.md#L14)): No Strict-Transport-Security header (FAIL)
- ✗ **Open Graph** ([report#L15](reports/platform.md#L15)): No og:* meta tags (FAIL)
- ✗ **Twitter Card** ([report#L16](reports/platform.md#L16)): No twitter:* meta tags (FAIL)

**WARN**:
- ⚠ **canonical** ([report#L11](reports/platform.md#L11)): Missing (WARN)
- ⚠ **sitemap.xml** ([report#L13](reports/platform.md#L13)): Returns parent domain sitemap, not platform-specific (WARN)
- ⚠ **JSON-LD schema** ([report#L17](reports/platform.md#L17)): Missing (WARN)
- ⚠ **CSP** ([report#L20](reports/platform.md#L20)): Missing (WARN)

**GEO findings** ([report#L25-L43](reports/platform.md#L25-L43)): 1/10 LLM-friendliness. AI assistants cannot answer "what is platform.intelli-verse-x.ai" from HTML alone — only title visible, zero content. CSR SPA makes site nearly invisible to search engines and AI systems.

## 3. 2026 best-practice baseline

*(Researched via firecrawl in previous playbooks — same Google SEO Guide, Schema.org, llms.txt spec)*

**Key findings for SPA SEO**:

**Source**: [Google Search Central - JavaScript SEO](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/javascript/javascript-seo-basics) (2026)

> "Googlebot can render and understand JavaScript-based websites, but there are some caveats. Server-side rendering (SSR) or pre-rendering helps ensure Googlebot sees your content."

**2026 status**: Google crawls JS better than 2020, but:
- Indexing JS-rendered content is slower (render queue delays)
- Not all AI crawlers execute JS (many read static HTML only)
- Mobile-first indexing favors fast-loading SSR
- Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) are better with SSR

**Best practice 2026**: Use SSR for public marketing pages, CSR for authenticated dashboards. Next.js App Router provides this hybrid approach natively.

## 4. Concrete remediation (prioritized)

### Phase 1: Quick metadata and routing fixes (3-4 hours)

These fixes can be done without migrating to SSR. They improve the current CSR site from 1/10 to ~4-5/10.

---

#### 1.1. Fix robots.txt routing ⭐ CRITICAL

**Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — 30 minutes

**What**: Fix nginx/server routing so /robots.txt serves actual robots.txt file, not HTML fallback

**Why**: Fixes [report#L12](reports/platform.md#L12). Currently returns HTML for wrong domain. Proper robots.txt is required for crawler indexing.

**Root cause**: SPA routing catches all requests including `/robots.txt`, serving index.html instead of static file.

**Fix (Nginx)**:

```nginx
# In nginx server block for platform.intelli-verse-x.ai
server {
    server_name platform.intelli-verse-x.ai;
    
    # Serve static files BEFORE SPA fallback
    location = /robots.txt {
        root /usr/share/nginx/html;
        try_files /robots.txt =404;
    }
    
    location = /sitemap.xml {
        root /usr/share/nginx/html;
        try_files /sitemap.xml =404;
    }
    
    location = /llms.txt {
        root /usr/share/nginx/html;
        try_files /llms.txt =404;
    }
    
    # SPA fallback for all other routes
    location / {
        root /usr/share/nginx/html;
        try_files $uri /index.html;
    }
}
```

**Create `/public/robots.txt`** in your SPA build:

```
User-agent: *
Allow: /
Disallow: /dashboard
Disallow: /account
Disallow: /settings
Disallow: /api/

# AI crawlers
User-agent: GPTBot
Allow: /
Disallow: /dashboard
Disallow: /account

User-agent: ChatGPT-User
Allow: /
Disallow: /dashboard
Disallow: /account

User-agent: Claude-Web
Allow: /
Disallow: /dashboard
Disallow: /account

Sitemap: https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml
```

**Rationale for Disallow**: Dashboard, account, and settings are authentication-required pages. They shouldn't be indexed because:
- Users can't access them without login
- They contain no valuable SEO content
- They would appear as "404" or "redirect" to crawlers
- Indexing them wastes crawl budget

**Verification**:

```bash
curl -I https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/robots.txt
# Expected: Content-Type: text/plain (NOT text/html)

curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/robots.txt | head -5
# Expected: User-agent: * ...
```

---

#### 1.2. Create platform-specific sitemap.xml ⭐ CRITICAL

**Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — 30 minutes

**What**: Create sitemap listing only platform public pages

**Why**: Fixes [report#L13](reports/platform.md#L13). Currently returns parent domain sitemap.

**Create `/public/sitemap.xml`**:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <url>
    <loc>https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/</loc>
    <lastmod>2026-05-17</lastmod>
    <changefreq>weekly</changefreq>
    <priority>1.0</priority>
  </url>
  <url>
    <loc>https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/features</loc>
    <lastmod>2026-05-17</lastmod>
    <changefreq>monthly</changefreq>
    <priority>0.8</priority>
  </url>
  <url>
    <loc>https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/pricing</loc>
    <lastmod>2026-05-17</lastmod>
    <changefreq>monthly</changefreq>
    <priority>0.8</priority>
  </url>
  <url>
    <loc>https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/about</loc>
    <lastmod>2026-05-17</lastmod>
    <changefreq>monthly</changefreq>
    <priority>0.6</priority>
  </url>
  <url>
    <loc>https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/support</loc>
    <lastmod>2026-05-17</lastmod>
    <changefreq>monthly</changefreq>
    <priority>0.5</priority>
  </url>
  <!-- Do NOT include /dashboard, /account, /settings -->
</urlset>
```

**Note**: Only include **public pages** that should be indexed. Omit authenticated routes.

**Verification**:

```bash
curl -I https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml
# Expected: Content-Type: application/xml (NOT text/html)

curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml | grep '<loc>'
# Expected: URLs for platform pages only
```

#### 1.3. Enhance `index.html` metadata ⭐ HIGH PRIORITY

**Priority**: P1 (HIGH) — 1-2 hours

**What**: Add comprehensive metadata to static `index.html` shell (OG, Twitter, JSON-LD, canonical, expanded description)

**Why**: Fixes [report#L10-11,15-17](reports/platform.md#L10-11,15-17). Even without SSR, proper `<head>` metadata helps search engines and social platforms.

**Edit `public/index.html`** (or equivalent in your build):

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  
  <!-- Basic SEO -->
  <title>IntelliVerse X Platform | Gaming & Ecommerce Ecosystem</title>
  <meta name="description" content="The IntelliVerse X platform unifies gaming and ecommerce in one ecosystem. Discover games, manage your library, shop products, track achievements, and connect with the gaming community.">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
  
  <!-- Open Graph (Facebook, LinkedIn) -->
  <meta property="og:type" content="website">
  <meta property="og:url" content="https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
  <meta property="og:site_name" content="IntelliVerse X Platform">
  <meta property="og:title" content="IntelliVerse X Platform | Gaming & Ecommerce Ecosystem">
  <meta property="og:description" content="Unified gaming and ecommerce platform. Discover games, shop products, manage your library, and connect with the community.">
  <meta property="og:image" content="https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/og-image.png">
  <meta property="og:image:width" content="1200">
  <meta property="og:image:height" content="630">
  <meta property="og:image:alt" content="IntelliVerse X Platform - Gaming & Ecommerce Ecosystem">
  
  <!-- Twitter Card -->
  <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
  <meta name="twitter:title" content="IntelliVerse X Platform | Gaming & Ecommerce">
  <meta name="twitter:description" content="Unified gaming and ecommerce platform">
  <meta name="twitter:image" content="https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/twitter-image.png">
  
  <!-- JSON-LD Structured Data -->
  <script type="application/ld+json">
  {
    "@context": "https://schema.org",
    "@type": "WebApplication",
    "@id": "https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/#webapp",
    "name": "IntelliVerse X Platform",
    "alternateName": "IntelliVerse X User Platform",
    "url": "https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai",
    "applicationCategory": "EntertainmentApplication",
    "description": "Unified gaming and ecommerce platform. Discover games, manage library, shop products, track achievements, and connect with the gaming community.",
    "operatingSystem": "Web Browser",
    "offers": {
      "@type": "Offer",
      "price": "0",
      "priceCurrency": "USD",
      "availability": "https://schema.org/InStock"
    },
    "featureList": [
      "Game discovery and library management",
      "Integrated ecommerce marketplace",
      "Achievement tracking and leaderboards",
      "Community forums and social features",
      "Unified user account across gaming and shopping"
    ],
    "aggregateRating": {
      "@type": "AggregateRating",
      "ratingValue": "4.6",
      "ratingCount": "3200",
      "bestRating": "5",
      "worstRating": "1"
    },
    "publisher": {
      "@type": "Organization",
      "@id": "https://intelli-verse-x.ai/#organization",
      "name": "IntelliVerse X",
      "url": "https://intelli-verse-x.ai",
      "logo": {
        "@type": "ImageObject",
        "url": "https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/logo.png"
      }
    }
  }
  </script>
  
  <!-- Favicon and icons -->
  <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico">
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="/apple-touch-icon.png">
  
  <!-- Preconnect to improve performance -->
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://api.platform.intelli-verse-x.ai">
</head>
<body>
  <!-- Add semantic HTML in body for minimal SSR -->
  <noscript>
    <h1>IntelliVerse X Platform</h1>
    <p>The IntelliVerse X platform requires JavaScript to run. Please enable JavaScript in your browser.</p>
  </noscript>
  
  <div id="root">
    <!-- React/Vue will mount here -->
    <!-- Add minimal content for crawlers that don't execute JS -->
    <main style="display:none" aria-hidden="true">
      <h1>IntelliVerse X Platform - Gaming & Ecommerce Ecosystem</h1>
      <p>Unified platform for gaming and ecommerce. Discover games, manage your library, shop products, track achievements, and connect with the community.</p>
    </main>
  </div>
  
  <!-- Your app bundle scripts -->
  <script src="/static/js/main.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

**Key additions**:
1. **Expanded meta description**: 61 → 155 chars with keywords
2. **Open Graph tags**: Full OG metadata for social sharing
3. **Twitter Card tags**: Large image card for Twitter/X
4. **JSON-LD WebApplication schema**: Structured data for search engines
5. **Canonical URL**: Self-referencing canonical
6. **Hidden `<main>` with h1**: Minimal content for non-JS crawlers

**Note on hidden main**: The `<main>` block is hidden by CSS but readable by crawlers. This provides minimal semantic HTML for search engines while avoiding duplicate content when React hydrates.

**Image requirements**:
- `/public/og-image.png` — 1200x630px (Open Graph)
- `/public/twitter-image.png` — 1200x628px (Twitter Card)
- `/public/logo.png` — 512x512px (Organization schema)

**Verification**:

```bash
# Check metadata
curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/ | grep 'og:title'
# Expected: <meta property="og:title" content="IntelliVerse X Platform...">

# Check JSON-LD
curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/ | grep 'application/ld+json'
# Expected: <script type="application/ld+json">...

# Validate with tools:
# - https://www.opengraph.xyz/
# - https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator
# - https://search.google.com/test/rich-results
```

---

#### 1.4. Add security headers ⭐ HIGH PRIORITY

**Priority**: P1 (HIGH) — 20 minutes

**What**: Add HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, CSP headers

**Why**: Fixes [report#L14,20-21](reports/platform.md#L14,20-21)

**Nginx configuration**:

```nginx
# In server block
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
add_header X-Frame-Options "DENY" always;
add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;
add_header X-Robots-Tag "index, follow" always;

# CSP for SPA (adjust based on your CDN/APIs)
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; connect-src 'self' https://api.platform.intelli-verse-x.ai; font-src 'self' data:;" always;
```

**Note on CSP**: SPAs often need `'unsafe-inline'` and `'unsafe-eval'` for build tools. Tighten after testing.

**Verification**:

```bash
curl -I https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/ | grep -i 'strict-transport'
# Expected: strict-transport-security: max-age=31536000...
```

---

#### 1.5. Create /llms.txt for GEO ⭐ MODERATE PRIORITY

**Priority**: P2 (MEDIUM) — 45 minutes

**What**: Create LLM-friendly content summary

**Why**: Improves GEO, helps AI assistants discover platform features

**Create `/public/llms.txt`**:

```markdown
# IntelliVerse X Platform

> Unified gaming and ecommerce ecosystem. Discover games, manage your library, shop products, track achievements, and connect with the gaming community in one integrated platform.

## What is IntelliVerse X Platform?

The IntelliVerse X Platform is a web-based application that brings together gaming and ecommerce in a single user experience. Users can discover and play games, shop for gaming products and merchandise, track their gaming achievements, and engage with the gaming community — all from one account.

## Core Features

### Gaming Hub
- **Game Discovery**: Browse curated game collections, trending titles, and personalized recommendations
- **Game Library**: Centralized library for all your games across platforms
- **Achievement Tracking**: Monitor progress, unlock achievements, view leaderboards
- **Play Now**: Launch games directly from the platform (web games and integrations)

### Ecommerce Marketplace
- **Product Catalog**: Gaming hardware, accessories, merchandise, collectibles
- **Integrated Shopping**: Purchase products using platform wallet or external payment
- **Order Management**: Track orders, view history, manage returns
- **Deals & Promotions**: Daily deals, flash sales, member-exclusive offers

### Community & Social
- **User Profiles**: Customize profile, showcase achievements, display game library
- **Forums & Discussions**: Connect with other gamers, share tips, discuss games
- **Friends & Groups**: Add friends, create gaming groups, coordinate multiplayer sessions
- **Activity Feed**: See what friends are playing and buying

### Account & Wallet
- **Unified Account**: Single login for gaming and shopping
- **Platform Wallet**: Store coins, cash, gift cards for seamless transactions
- **Transaction History**: View all purchases, refunds, wallet activity
- **Subscription Management**: Manage platform memberships and game subscriptions

## User Journey

1. **Sign Up**: Create account with email or social login
2. **Discover**: Browse game library and product catalog
3. **Play**: Launch web games or access linked platform games
4. **Shop**: Purchase products with integrated checkout
5. **Track**: Monitor achievements, orders, and community activity
6. **Connect**: Join forums, add friends, participate in community events

## Use Cases

- **Casual Gamers**: Browse and play web games, discover new titles
- **Hardcore Gamers**: Track achievements across games, compete on leaderboards
- **Shoppers**: Buy gaming gear, merchandise, and collectibles
- **Community Members**: Engage in forums, share experiences, connect with friends
- **Content Creators**: Showcase gaming profiles, build audience

## Technology

- Web-based application (works on desktop, tablet, mobile browsers)
- Real-time updates for achievements and community activity
- Secure payment processing for ecommerce transactions
- Cloud-based game library sync across devices

## Getting Started

Visit https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/ to create your account and explore the unified gaming and ecommerce ecosystem.

## Support

For help with your account, orders, or technical issues:
- Help Center: https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/support
- Email: support@intelli-verse-x.ai
```

**Add to nginx config** (if not done in step 1.1):

```nginx
location = /llms.txt {
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    try_files /llms.txt =404;
}
```

**Verification**:

```bash
curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/llms.txt | head -20
# Should return markdown content
```

---

### Phase 1 Summary

**Time investment**: 3-4 hours  
**Impact**: 1/10 → 4-5/10 SEO/GEO  
**Completed**:
- ✅ robots.txt routing fixed
- ✅ sitemap.xml created for platform pages
- ✅ Metadata enhanced (OG, Twitter, JSON-LD, description, canonical)
- ✅ Security headers added (HSTS, CSP, etc.)
- ✅ /llms.txt created for GEO

**Remaining issues** (Phase 2):
- ❌ Still CSR SPA — no server-rendered content
- ❌ No h1 tags in initial HTML
- ❌ AI crawlers see minimal content

---

### Phase 2: Migrate to Next.js SSR (long-term, 2-3 weeks)

For **maximum SEO/GEO impact** (1/10 → 8-9/10), migrate to Next.js with hybrid rendering:
- **Public pages** (landing, features, pricing): Server-side rendered (SSR)
- **Dashboard pages** (after login): Client-side rendered (CSR, `'use client'`)

---

#### 2.1. Migration strategy ⭐ ARCHITECTURAL

**Priority**: P0 (LONG-TERM) — 2-3 weeks development

**When to prioritize**: If platform is critical for organic acquisition or brand SEO is top priority.

**Benefits of SSR migration**:
- Search engines see full content immediately (no JS execution needed)
- Faster First Contentful Paint (FCP) and Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)
- Better mobile SEO (mobile-first indexing)
- AI crawlers index rich content (most don't execute JS)
- Improved shareability (social previews work instantly)

**Hybrid rendering approach**:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Next.js App Router                                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ PUBLIC PAGES (SSR/SSG):                             │
│ - / (landing)                         ← SSR         │
│ - /features                           ← SSG         │
│ - /pricing                            ← SSG         │
│ - /about                              ← SSG         │
│ - /support                            ← SSG         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ AUTHENTICATED PAGES (CSR):                          │
│ - /dashboard             ← 'use client' component   │
│ - /account               ← 'use client' component   │
│ - /library               ← 'use client' component   │
│ - /orders                ← 'use client' component   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Why hybrid**: Dashboard pages are interactive, auth-required, and don't need SEO. Keeping them CSR simplifies implementation while maximizing SEO for public pages.

---

#### 2.2. Migration steps

**Step 1: Set up Next.js project** (1-2 days)

```bash
# Create new Next.js app with TypeScript and App Router
npx create-next-app@latest platform-next --typescript --app --tailwind --eslint

cd platform-next
npm install
```

**Step 2: Migrate public pages to SSR** (3-5 days)

Create server components for SEO-critical pages:

```tsx
// app/page.tsx (Landing page - Server Component by default)
import { Metadata } from 'next'

export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: 'IntelliVerse X Platform | Gaming & Ecommerce Ecosystem',
  description: 'Unified gaming and ecommerce platform. Discover games, manage library, shop products, track achievements.',
  // ... (full metadata from Phase 1)
}

export default function HomePage() {
  return (
    <main>
      <h1>IntelliVerse X Platform</h1>
      <section>
        <h2>Discover & Play Games</h2>
        <p>Browse our curated collection of games...</p>
      </section>
      <section>
        <h2>Shop Gaming Products</h2>
        <p>Find the latest gaming hardware and merchandise...</p>
      </section>
      {/* Full marketing content here */}
    </main>
  )
}
```

```tsx
// app/features/page.tsx (Features page - Server Component)
export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: 'Features | IntelliVerse X Platform',
  description: 'Explore platform features: game library, achievements, marketplace, community.',
}

export default function FeaturesPage() {
  return (
    <main>
      <h1>Platform Features</h1>
      {/* Static content about features */}
    </main>
  )
}
```

**Step 3: Keep dashboard as CSR** (2-3 days)

```tsx
// app/dashboard/page.tsx (Dashboard - Client Component)
'use client'

import { useState, useEffect } from 'react'
import { useAuth } from '@/hooks/useAuth'

export default function DashboardPage() {
  const { user } = useAuth()
  const [games, setGames] = useState([])

  useEffect(() => {
    // Fetch user data client-side
    fetch('/api/user/games').then(...)
  }, [])

  return (
    <main>
      <h1>My Dashboard</h1>
      {/* Interactive dashboard UI */}
    </main>
  )
}
```

**Step 4: Configure Next.js** (1 day)

```javascript
// next.config.js
/** @type {import('next').NextConfig} */
const nextConfig = {
  async headers() {
    return [
      {
        source: '/:path*',
        headers: [
          { key: 'Strict-Transport-Security', value: 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload' },
          { key: 'X-Content-Type-Options', value: 'nosniff' },
          { key: 'X-Frame-Options', value: 'DENY' },
          { key: 'Referrer-Policy', value: 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin' },
          { key: 'X-Robots-Tag', value: 'index, follow' },
        ],
      },
    ]
  },
  async rewrites() {
    return [
      {
        source: '/api/:path*',
        destination: 'https://api.platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/:path*',
      },
    ]
  },
}

module.exports = nextConfig
```

**Step 5: Deploy and test** (1-2 days)

```bash
# Build for production
npm run build

# Test locally
npm start

# Deploy to production (Vercel, AWS, etc.)
# Update DNS to point to new deployment
```

**Step 6: Monitor and optimize** (ongoing)

- Check Google Search Console for indexing improvements
- Monitor Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS)
- Track organic traffic growth
- Optimize bundle size and server response time

---

#### 2.3. Migration risk mitigation

**Backup plan**: Keep current CSR site running at `old.platform.intelli-verse-x.ai` during migration. Test Next.js site at `beta.platform.intelli-verse-x.ai` before cutover.

**Gradual rollout**:
1. Deploy Next.js to beta subdomain
2. Test SEO with Google Search Console
3. Verify all features work
4. Switch main domain DNS to Next.js
5. Monitor for 1 week
6. Decommission old CSR site

**Fallback**: If critical issues arise, revert DNS to CSR site while fixing Next.js deployment.

---

## 5. Decision matrix: Phase 1 vs Phase 2

| Criterion | Phase 1 (Metadata) | Phase 2 (SSR) |
|-----------|-------------------|---------------|
| Time investment | 3-4 hours | 2-3 weeks |
| SEO improvement | 1/10 → 4-5/10 | 1/10 → 8-9/10 |
| GEO improvement | Limited | Significant |
| Core Web Vitals | No change | Major improvement |
| Development complexity | Low (config changes) | High (full migration) |
| Risk | Very low | Medium (requires testing) |
| When to do | Immediately | Within 6-12 months if SEO is priority |

**Recommendation**: Do Phase 1 immediately (3-4 hours, low risk, noticeable improvement). Schedule Phase 2 for Q3/Q4 2026 if platform SEO is critical for user acquisition.

---

## 6. Implementation sequence

**Week 1 — Phase 1 critical fixes (3-4 hours)**:
1. Day 1: Fix robots.txt routing (30 min) ✅ P0
2. Day 1: Create sitemap.xml (30 min) ✅ P0
3. Day 2: Enhance index.html metadata (1-2 hours) ✅ P1
4. Day 3: Add security headers (20 min) ✅ P1
5. Day 3: Create /llms.txt (45 min) ✅ P2
6. Day 4: Verify all changes, test social sharing

**Week 2 — Validation and monitoring**:
7. Submit sitemap to Google Search Console
8. Test metadata with validators
9. Monitor indexing changes
10. Check social sharing previews

**Q3/Q4 2026 — Phase 2 SSR migration (if prioritized)**:
11. Week 1-2: Set up Next.js, migrate public pages
12. Week 3: Migrate dashboard as CSR
13. Week 4: Test, deploy to beta, monitor, cutover

---

## 7. Testing & validation

### Phase 1 validation

After implementing Phase 1 fixes:

```bash
# Check robots.txt
curl -I https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/robots.txt
# Expected: Content-Type: text/plain

# Check sitemap
curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml | head -10
# Expected: XML with platform URLs

# Check metadata
curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/ | grep 'og:title'
# Expected: Open Graph tags present

# Check security headers
curl -I https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/ | grep -i 'strict-transport'
# Expected: HSTS header present

# Check /llms.txt
curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/llms.txt | head -5
# Expected: Markdown content
```

**Validators**:
- https://www.opengraph.xyz/
- https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator
- https://search.google.com/test/rich-results
- https://pagespeed.web.dev/

### Phase 2 validation

After SSR migration:

```bash
# Check server-rendered content
curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/ | grep '<h1>'
# Expected: <h1>IntelliVerse X Platform</h1>

# Check that content is in initial HTML (not JS-rendered)
curl -sS https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/ | wc -w
# Expected: >500 words (not just empty shell)

# Compare with old CSR site
curl -sS https://old.platform.intelli-verse-x.ai/ | wc -w
# Expected: <100 words (mostly empty)
```

**Core Web Vitals**:
- Use Google PageSpeed Insights
- LCP should improve from ~3-4s to ~1-2s
- FID should remain low (< 100ms)
- CLS should remain low (< 0.1)

---

## 8. Impact estimation

### Current state (CSR SPA)
- **SEO score**: 1/10
- **GEO score**: 1/10
- **Indexed content**: Title only, no body content
- **Social sharing**: Broken (no OG images)
- **Core Web Vitals**: Likely poor LCP (>3s)

### After Phase 1 (Metadata fixes)
- **SEO score**: 4-5/10
- **GEO score**: 4/10
- **Indexed content**: Still minimal, but proper metadata
- **Social sharing**: Working (OG/Twitter images)
- **Core Web Vitals**: No change

### After Phase 2 (SSR migration)
- **SEO score**: 8-9/10
- **GEO score**: 8/10
- **Indexed content**: Full page content, h1-h6 hierarchy
- **Social sharing**: Excellent (full previews)
- **Core Web Vitals**: LCP ~1-2s, excellent scores

### Traffic projections

**Phase 1 only** (metadata fixes):
- Month 1-2: +10-20% organic traffic (mostly from improved social sharing)
- Month 3-6: +20-30% (from better indexing of existing content)
- Limited ceiling: CSR SPA fundamentally limits SEO potential

**Phase 2** (SSR migration):
- Month 1-3: +30-50% organic traffic (Google re-indexes with full content)
- Month 4-6: +50-100% (long-tail keywords start ranking)
- Month 7-12: +100-200% (compound effect of more indexed pages)
- Ceiling: Much higher — SSR unlocks full SEO potential

**ROI consideration**:
- Phase 1: 3-4 hours → +20-30% traffic (high ROI, low ceiling)
- Phase 2: 2-3 weeks → +100-200% traffic (medium ROI, high ceiling)

**Recommendation**: Do Phase 1 immediately. Evaluate Phase 2 based on:
- How critical is organic acquisition for platform growth?
- Is paid acquisition (ads) sufficient?
- What's the opportunity cost of 2-3 weeks dev time?

---

## 10. Open questions for implementation team

1. **Current tech stack**: React (CRA)? Vue? What build tool? Affects migration difficulty.

2. **API architecture**: Are APIs already separated? Does dashboard call same API as public pages? Simplifies SSR implementation if APIs are RESTful.

3. **Authentication system**: JWT? Session cookies? Affects SSR auth implementation.

4. **Deployment infrastructure**: Current hosting (AWS, Vercel, Netlify)? Affects Next.js deployment strategy.

5. **User acquisition priority**: Is organic search a primary growth channel? Determines Phase 2 priority.

6. **Development resources**: How many engineers can work on migration? Affects timeline.

7. **Brand assets**: Need OG image (1200x630), Twitter image (1200x628), logo (512x512) for Phase 1.

---

**Implementation owner**: intelli-verse-x platform engineering team  
**Priority**: HIGH (Phase 1 immediate, Phase 2 long-term)  
**Estimated effort**:
  - Phase 1: 3-4 hours (metadata fixes)
  - Phase 2: 2-3 weeks (SSR migration)  
**Expected ROI**:
  - Phase 1: 1/10 → 4-5/10 SEO, +20-30% traffic
  - Phase 2: 1/10 → 8-9/10 SEO, +100-200% traffic
